privacy_boundary: Public repository files and commit metadata only; no raw chat, health, pet-health, credentials, contact data, account identifiers, or unreviewed uploads.

memory_slice.title: Owner-scoped queue projections advanced beyond the canonical active queue

records.summary: The canonical operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json still records continuity item M-001 as the latest continuity item, while append-only continuity queue projections and commits establish completed work through M-005.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the cited canonical queue blob and the M-005 queue-projection commit; reject this observation only if the canonical file contains M-005 or a later continuity record at the cited blob.

records.summary: The two records are not mutually exclusive when interpreted by scope: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a stale canonical snapshot, while operations/queue-updates contains newer owner-scoped append-only projections. Neither source alone should be described as the complete current queue state.
contradicts: Any report treating ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone as the complete current queue
Any report treating an owner-scoped queue projection as if it had already updated the canonical queue
supersedes: The implicit assumption that append-only queue projections and canonical queue state are automatically synchronized
records.falsification_or_review_route: Re-evaluate when a reconciler commit updates the canonical queue from validated owner projections or when an authoritative queue-resolution rule is added.

records.summary: Queue readers should compute effective state from the canonical queue plus validated owner-scoped projections ordered by generated_at and commit ancestry, while preserving the canonical snapshot and every projection as immutable provenance. A reconciler may update the canonical file only after detecting duplicate IDs, owner mismatch, non-monotonic status, and conflicting source commits.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Implement a deterministic reconciler and test it against duplicate IDs, cross-owner writes, reversed timestamps, status regression, and projections whose artifact commits are absent.

records.summary: No inspected artifact defines which process is authorized to reconcile owner-scoped queue projections into operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, how conflicts are adjudicated, or whether the canonical queue is intended to be materialized state or only a bootstrap snapshot.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Resolve by committing an explicit queue reconciliation contract naming authority, merge order, validation rules, rollback behavior, and acceptance tests.
